title: Preface, Zechariah, Mary, and John’s birth
summary_text: Luke writes to Theophilus so he may know the certainty of what he was taught. Gabriel appears to Zechariah in the temple, promising John; Zechariah is struck mute for unbelief until the child is named. Gabriel greets Mary with news of the Son of the Most High; she visits Elizabeth, sings the Magnificat, and Elizabeth blesses her faith. John is born; Zechariah’s tongue is loosed as he names him John and prophesies the Benedictus about the horn of salvation and the dawn from on high.
key_points: Luke traced matters accurately from the first.
Zechariah doubted and could not speak until John was named.
Mary’s child would reign on David’s throne forever.
Mary magnified God for looking on her humble state.
Zechariah praised God for visiting and redeeming his people.
John grew strong in spirit in the wilderness.
evidence_phrases: that you might know the certainty
you will be silent
you have found favor with God
My soul magnifies the Lord
His name is John
Blessed be the Lord, the God of Israel
you, child, will be called a prophet of the Most High

title: Birth, shepherds, presentation, and boy Jesus
summary_text: Augustus’s census brings Joseph and Mary to Bethlehem where Jesus is born and laid in a manger for lack of room. Angels announce good tidings to shepherds who find the babe as sign. At eight days he is named and presented; Simeon blesses God having seen salvation and warns Mary of a sword; Anna gives thanks. Each year at Passover the boy Jesus stays behind in the temple, astounding teachers with his understanding, then submits to his parents and grows in wisdom.
key_points: The child was wrapped and laid in a feeding trough.
Today is born a Savior, who is Christ the Lord.
A multitude praised God: glory in the highest.
Simeon’s eyes saw God’s salvation prepared before all peoples.
A sword would pierce Mary’s soul.
Jesus said he must be about his Father’s things.
evidence_phrases: there was no room for them in the inn
there is born to you today a Savior
Glory to God in the highest
for my eyes have seen your salvation
yes, a sword will pierce through your own soul
Didn’t you know that I must be in my Father’s house?
Jesus increased in wisdom and stature

title: John’s preaching, Jesus’ baptism, and genealogy
summary_text: John son of Zechariah preaches repentance and a baptism of forgiveness in the Jordan wilderness, warning broods of vipers to bear fruit and announcing the mightier one with the winnowing fan. Jesus is baptized; heaven opens, the Spirit descends bodily, and the voice says you are my beloved Son. Luke then traces Jesus’s genealogy from Joseph back through David and Abraham to Adam, son of God.
themes: Son of Adam, son of God
key_points: John called for repentance to prepare the Lord’s way.
He warned the axe was already at the root of the trees.
Jesus was baptized while praying.
The Holy Spirit descended in bodily form as a dove.
The genealogy runs from Jesus to Adam.
evidence_phrases: You offspring of vipers
bring forth fruits worthy of repentance
he will baptize you in the Holy Spirit
You are my beloved Son
son of Enos, son of Seth, son of Adam, son of God

title: Temptation, Nazareth rejected, and Capernaum authority
summary_text: Full of the Holy Spirit, Jesus is tempted in the wilderness, answering the devil from Scripture. In Nazareth’s synagogue he reads Isaiah’s good-news prophecy, claims fulfillment, and is driven out when he cites Elijah and Elisha helping Gentiles. At Capernaum he teaches with authority, rebukes an unclean spirit, heals Peter’s mother-in-law and many sick and demonized, and preaches in other synagogues of Galilee.
themes: Man shall not live by bread alone
key_points: Jesus resisted every temptation with God’s word.
He said today this Scripture is fulfilled in your hearing.
They tried to throw him off the brow of the hill.
His word cast out a demon in the Capernaum synagogue.
He laid hands on the sick and they were healed.
evidence_phrases: Man shall not live by bread alone
The Spirit of the Lord is on me
Today, this Scripture has been fulfilled
no prophet is acceptable in his hometown
Be silent, and come out of him!

title: Calling disciples, cleansing, and conflict
summary_text: Jesus teaches from Simon’s boat then commands a catch of fish; Simon, James, and John leave all to follow. A leper is cleansed; Jesus withdraws to pray as crowds press. Friends lower a paralytic through a roof; Jesus forgives sins then heals him to prove Son of Man authority on earth. Levi leaves the tax office for a feast with sinners; Jesus defends physicians for the sick and new wine for new wineskins. Pharisees challenge Sabbath grain; Jesus declares the Son of Man Lord of the Sabbath.
key_points: Simon fell at Jesus’s knees after the great catch.
The leper was cleansed when Jesus willed it.
The paralytic walked, showing authority to forgive.
Levi made a feast for Jesus with other tax collectors.
The Son of Man is lord of the Sabbath.
evidence_phrases: from now on you will be catching people alive
Depart from me, for I am a sinful man, Lord
Man, your sins are forgiven you
I have not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance
The Son of Man is also lord of the Sabbath

title: Sabbath, the Twelve, and the sermon on the plain
summary_text: Jesus defends plucking grain and healing a withered hand on the Sabbath against Pharisees and scribes. He spends the night in prayer, chooses twelve apostles, and heals a great crowd on a level place. He pronounces blessings and woes, teaches love for enemies, judging others, good trees and fruit, building on rock by hearing and doing his words.
themes: Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath
key_points: It is lawful on the Sabbath to do good.
Jesus chose twelve whom he also named apostles.
Blessed are the poor, hungry, weeping, hated for the Son of Man.
Love enemies, bless those who curse you.
The wise builder hears Jesus’s words and does them.
evidence_phrases: the Son of Man is lord of the Sabbath
he chose twelve, whom he also named apostles
Blessed are you who are poor
love your enemies
like a man building a house, who dug and laid a foundation on the rock

title: Centurion, widow’s son, John’s question, and forgiven woman
summary_text: A centurion’s faith amazes Jesus as his servant is healed at a word. Jesus raises a widow’s only son at Nain. John’s disciples hear of Jesus’s works; Jesus points to healings and good news to the poor as signs and praises John yet says least in the Kingdom is greater. A Pharisee’s dinner guest is a sinful woman who washes Jesus’s feet with tears; he forgives her many sins for great love.
themes: Young man, I tell you, arise
key_points: Jesus marveled at the centurion’s faith.
The bier-bearer sat up when Jesus spoke.
John asked if another should be looked for.
Wisdom is justified by all her children.
Her many sins were forgiven because she loved much.
evidence_phrases: I tell you, I have not found such great faith
Young man, I tell you, arise!
Are you the one who is coming, or should we look for another?
Wisdom is justified by all her children
Your sins are forgiven
Your faith has saved you. Go in peace

title: Parables, storm, demoniac, Jairus, and bleeding woman
summary_text: Jesus teaches the sower parable and explains soils; a lamp belongs on a stand and true family hears God’s word. He stills a storm; the Gerasene demoniac Legion is cleansed and sent to tell what God did. Jairus’s daughter dies while a woman touches Jesus’s garment and is healed; Jesus raises the child saying she is not dead but sleeping.
themes: Your faith has made you well
key_points: The seed on good soil bears fruit with patience.
Those who hear God’s word and do it are Jesus’s mother and brothers.
Jesus rebuked wind and raging water.
The demoniac was clothed and in his right mind.
The woman was healed immediately by faith.
The child arose at Jesus’s call.
evidence_phrases: in the good soil, these are those
My mother and my brothers are these who hear the word of God
Where is your faith?
What is your name?
Someone did touch me
Don’t be afraid. Only believe
Child, arise!

title: Mission, feeding five thousand, transfiguration, and cost
summary_text: Jesus sends the twelve to preach and heal; Herod wonders who Jesus is. Five thousand are fed; Peter confesses Christ; Jesus foretells suffering. On the mountain Moses and Elijah appear; the Father says hear him. A boy’s demon is cast out after the father’s cry for help. Greatness is receiving a child; whoever is not against them is for them. Samaritans refuse Jesus; would-be followers learn the cost of following without looking back.
themes: No one who puts his hand to the plow
key_points: The twelve preached the Kingdom and healed.
Jesus fed five thousand with five loaves.
Peter said the Christ of God.
The Father’s voice commanded listening to the Son.
Foxes have holes but the Son of Man has nowhere to lay his head.
evidence_phrases: Take nothing for your journey
The Christ of God
This is my beloved Son. Listen to him!
I believe. Help my unbelief!
No one, having put his hand to the plow

title: Seventy sent, Samaritan, and Martha and Mary
summary_text: Jesus appoints seventy others, sends them as lambs among wolves with peace and judgment on rejecting cities, and rejoices that Satan falls as they return with joy. A lawyer’s who is my neighbor is answered by the Good Samaritan who showed mercy. Martha is distracted with much serving while Mary sits at Jesus’s feet hearing his word—the good part will not be taken away.
key_points: The harvest is plentiful; laborers are few.
Jesus saw Satan fall from heaven like lightning.
The Samaritan bound wounds and paid the innkeeper.
Go and do likewise was the neighbor lesson.
Mary chose the good part that will not be taken away.
evidence_phrases: The harvest is indeed plentiful, but the laborers are few
don’t rejoice … but rejoice that your names are written in heaven
A certain Samaritan, as he traveled
Go and do likewise
one thing is needed
Mary has chosen the good part

title: Prayer, Beelzebul, sign of Jonah, and woes
summary_text: Jesus teaches the Lord’s Prayer and persistence in asking; the strong man is bound so spoil is taken. Blasphemy against the Spirit is warned; Jonah is the sign to Nineveh. A lamp is the eye’s light; Pharisees are inwardly full of greed. Woes hit Pharisees for tithing mint while neglecting justice and love and for loading burdens; lawyers are blamed for taking away the key of knowledge.
key_points: The disciples prayed as Jesus taught.
If Satan is divided his kingdom cannot stand.
The sign of Jonah was given to this generation.
Clean the inside of the cup, not just the outside.
You lawyers load men with burdens hard to bear.
evidence_phrases: When you pray, say
everyone who asks receives
if Satan also is divided against himself
This generation will not have a sign, except Jonah
Woe to you Pharisees!
you took away the key of knowledge

title: Fear God, rich fool, anxiety, and readiness
summary_text: Jesus warns leaven of Pharisees—hypocrisy will be revealed. Fear him who after killing has authority to cast into Gehenna; the Spirit will teach you. The rich fool stores barns but dies that night. Birds and lilies teach trust; seek God’s Kingdom. Be dressed for service like waiting servants; the Son of Man comes at an hour you don’t expect. Fire and division on earth; interpreting the present time.
themes: Nothing covered that won’t be revealed
Fear him who casts into Gehenna
This night your soul is required
Ready for the Son of Man
key_points: Hypocrisy will be shouted from housetops.
Do not fear those who kill the body.
God clothes the grass; how much more you.
Sell possessions and give alms; treasure in heaven.
The Son of Man comes like a thief in the night.
evidence_phrases: There is nothing covered up that will not be revealed
I will warn you whom you should fear
This night your soul is required of you
where your treasure is, there your heart will be also
be ready also, for the Son of Man is coming
You hypocrites! You know how to interpret … the earth and the sky

title: Repent, healing on Sabbath, narrow door, and Jerusalem
summary_text: Unless you repent you will perish like the Galileans and tower victims. The barren fig tree gets one more year. Jesus heals a bent woman on the Sabbath; the ruler is put to shame. The Kingdom is like mustard and leaven. Strive to enter the narrow door; many will seek to enter when it is too late. Herod is a fox; Jesus laments Jerusalem unwilling under his wings as a hen gathers chicks.
key_points: Tower and Galilean deaths call for repentance unless you likewise perish.
The woman stood straight glorifying God.
Some are last who will be first.
Many will knock when the master has shut the door.
How often I wanted to gather your children!
evidence_phrases: unless you repent, you will all perish
Ought not this woman, being a daughter of Abraham
there are last who will be first
Strive to enter in by the narrow door
your house is left to you desolate

title: Humility, banquet, and counting the cost
summary_text: At a Pharisee’s feast Jesus teaches taking the lowest seat and inviting the poor who cannot repay. The great supper parable shows invited guests making excuses while the poor and lame fill the hall. Discipleship costs bearing the cross, hating family in comparison, renouncing possessions, and finishing the tower and war you begin; salt must not lose savor.
key_points: Whoever exalts himself will be humbled.
The master sent to highways and hedges to fill his house.
Whoever does not bear his cross cannot be disciple.
Count the cost like a tower builder or king going to war.
Salt is good, but if it becomes tasteless it is worthless.
evidence_phrases: everyone who exalts himself will be humbled
they all alike began to make excuses
Go out into the highways and hedges, and compel them to come in
whoever doesn’t renounce all that he has can’t be my disciple
Salt is good

title: Lost sheep, coin, and prodigal son
summary_text: Jesus receives sinners; Pharisees murmur. He tells three parables: the shepherd leaves ninety-nine for one lost sheep; the woman searches for one lost coin; the father runs to the returning prodigal while the elder brother resents the feast. Heaven rejoices over one sinner repenting more than over ninety-nine righteous needing no repentance.
themes: He was lost, and is found
key_points: There will be more joy in heaven over one repenting sinner.
The father saw his son while still far off and ran.
The feast celebrated he was dead and is alive again.
The elder son refused to enter the celebration.
All the father has belongs to the older son.
evidence_phrases: there will be more joy in heaven over one sinner who repents
Rejoice with me, for I have found my sheep
this my son was dead, and is alive again
let us eat and be merry
all that I have is yours

title: Unjust steward and rich man and Lazarus
summary_text: The unjust steward is commended for worldly prudence; make friends by unrighteous mammon for eternal dwellings. Law and prophets were until John; the Kingdom is preached. Divorce and remarriage are taught strictly. The rich man ignored beggar Lazarus at his gate; after death roles reverse across a great gulf; Moses and the prophets are enough if men will not hear them.
themes: They have Moses and the prophets
key_points: The children of this world are wiser than children of the light.
You cannot serve God and mammon.
The Law and the Prophets were until John.
The rich man begged for Lazarus to dip his finger in water.
If they do not hear Moses and the prophets, neither will they be persuaded.
evidence_phrases: the lord commended the dishonest manager
You can’t serve both God and mammon
The law and the prophets were until John
Son, remember that you received your good things
They have Moses and the prophets

title: Forgiveness, faith, ten lepers, and the coming Kingdom
summary_text: Jesus teaches forgiving a brother seven times a day when he repents and says faith as a mustard seed uproots trees. Ten lepers are cleansed as they go; only a Samaritan returns praising God. Pharisees ask when the Kingdom comes; Jesus says it is within you or among you. Days of the Son of Man will be like Noah and Lot; one taken, one left—do not look back.
key_points: If a brother repents seven times in a day, forgive.
We have only done our duty when we obey.
Only the foreigner returned to give thanks.
The Kingdom does not come with observation.
Whoever seeks to save his life will lose it.
evidence_phrases: If he sins against you seven times … forgive him
We are unworthy servants
Weren’t the ten cleansed? Where are the nine?
the Kingdom of God is within you
Remember Lot’s wife!

title: Persistent widow, Pharisee and tax collector, children, ruler, blind
summary_text: The unjust judge yields to a widow’s persistence—God will avenge his elect speedily. A tax collector justified beats his breast far off while a Pharisee trusts himself; whoever exalts himself is humbled. Jesus receives little children as Kingdom receivers. A rich ruler cannot sell all; how hard for the rich to enter—what is impossible with men is possible with God. A blind beggar by Jericho receives sight and follows glorifying God.
key_points: Men ought always to pray and not give up.
The Pharisee thanked God he was not like others.
The tax collector went down justified.
Whoever does not receive God’s Kingdom as a child will not enter.
The things impossible with men are possible with God.
The blind man’s faith made him well.
evidence_phrases: always pray, and don’t give up
will God not avenge his chosen ones?
everyone who exalts himself will be humbled
whoever doesn’t receive God’s Kingdom like a little child
The things which are impossible with men are possible with God
Receive your sight. Your faith has healed you

title: Zacchaeus, pounds, and weeping over Jerusalem
summary_text: Zacchaeus climbs a tree; Jesus says salvation has come to his house today. The parable of pounds rebukes those who hid the mina versus the faithful investor. Jesus rides a colt into Jerusalem as king; Pharisees want him to rebuke the crowd—if these were silent, stones would cry out. He weeps over Jerusalem for not knowing the things that make for peace.
key_points: Today salvation came to Zacchaeus’s house.
The nobleman went to receive a kingdom and demanded account.
Stone would cry out if disciples were silent.
Jesus wept over the city that did not know its visitation.
He cast out buyers from the temple.
evidence_phrases: Today, salvation has come to this house
Occupy until I come
Blessed is the King who comes
if these were silent, the stones would cry out
If you, even you, had known today the things which belong to peace!
My house is a house of prayer

title: Authority, vineyard, Caesar, and David’s son
summary_text: Chief priests, scribes, and elders question Jesus’s authority; he answers with John’s baptism. The vineyard parable shows tenants killing the beloved son; the stone the builders rejected becomes head. Render to Caesar and to God. Sadducees fail on resurrection marriage; God is God of the living. David’s son is David’s Lord in the Spirit.
key_points: They could not tell if John’s baptism was from heaven or men.
The heir was cast out of the vineyard and killed.
Give Caesar what bears his image.
God is not God of the dead but of the living.
How is Christ David’s son if David calls him Lord?
evidence_phrases: The baptism of John, was it from heaven, or from men?
let’s kill him, that the inheritance may be ours
Render to Caesar
He is not the God of the dead, but of the living
David therefore calls him Lord

title: Widow’s offering and Olivet prophecy
summary_text: The poor widow’s two mites outvalue rich gifts. Jesus foretells temple stones cast down, wars, earthquakes, persecution, Jerusalem surrounded, distress of nations, and the Son of Man coming in a cloud with power. The fig tree lesson and watchfulness apply: escape and stand before the Son of Man.
key_points: She gave all her living.
Days will come when not one stone will be left on another.
They will lay hands on you and persecute.
Jerusalem will be trampled by Gentiles until times fulfilled.
When these things begin, look up—your redemption draws near.
evidence_phrases: she gave out of her poverty
not one stone will be left on another
this will be your opportunity to testify
Jerusalem will be trampled down by the Gentiles
then they will see the Son of Man coming in a cloud
your redemption is near

title: Last supper, betrayal, and arrest
summary_text: The Passover plot; Judas agrees to betray. Jesus institutes bread and cup as his body and blood for remembrance; a dispute over greatness is answered with serving as the greatest. Peter’s denial is foretold. Gethsemane prayer in agony yields not my will but yours. Judas betrays with a kiss; Peter cuts off an ear then denies Jesus three times before the rooster.
themes: I am among you as one who serves
key_points: Jesus eagerly desired to eat this Passover before suffering.
The cup is the new covenant in his blood.
Let the greatest become as the youngest, the leader as servant.
His sweat became like great drops of blood.
Peter wept bitterly after denying three times.
evidence_phrases: Do this in memory of me
the new covenant in my blood
I am among you as one who serves
Nevertheless, not my will, but yours, be done
Judas, do you betray the Son of Man with a kiss?
Before the rooster crows today, you will deny me three times

title: Trials, crucifixion, and burial
summary_text: Jesus is tried before Pilate and Herod, declared innocent yet delivered by mob will. Simon carries the cross; women mourn; Jesus says weep for Jerusalem. Two criminals are crucified with him; one mocks, one asks remembrance in the Kingdom. Darkness covers; Jesus commits his spirit and the centurion glorifies God. Joseph buries the body in a tomb where no one had lain.
key_points: Pilate and Herod found no capital crime in him.
Jesus asked the Father to forgive those crucifying him.
The repentant thief was promised paradise that day.
The sun failed; the temple veil tore.
Joseph wrapped him in linen in an unused tomb.
evidence_phrases: I found no fault in this man
Father, forgive them, for they don’t know what they are doing
Assuredly I tell you, today you will be with me in Paradise
Father, into your hands I commend my spirit
Certainly this was a righteous man
laid it in a tomb that was cut in stone

title: Resurrection, Emmaus, and blessing at Bethany
summary_text: Women find the empty tomb; two angels remind them of the third-day prediction. On the Emmaus road Jesus opens Scripture; they recognize him in breaking bread. He appears to the eleven, shows flesh and bones, eats fish, and opens law and prophets and psalms concerning himself. They are witnesses of repentance and forgiveness to all nations; he blesses them as he is carried up to heaven.
themes: Why seek the living among the dead?
key_points: He is not here but risen as he said.
Moses and all the prophets spoke of Christ’s suffering and glory.
Repentance and forgiveness should be preached in his name.
You are witnesses of these things.
I send the promise of my Father upon you.
He was carried up while blessing them.
evidence_phrases: Why do you seek the living among the dead?
Didn’t our hearts burn within us?
It is I myself. Touch me and see
Thus it is written, and thus it was necessary
You are witnesses of these things
while he blessed them, he withdrew from them
